data.body:
=== DESIGN OF RECORD: plan-of-record-v4.md ===

# rebar Plan-Review Gate — DESIGN OF RECORD (v4)

A verification workflow that runs when a ticket is claimed (open->in_progress), reviewing the ticket's
implementation plan BEFORE an agent executes it — the inverse of the completion-verifier close gate. A rebar
FEATURE used by a BROAD, DIVERSE CLIENT BASE (many languages/frameworks/structures + many ticket-creation
styles). Supersedes our-review-dimensions-v3.md. Full dimension detail: dso-dimension-catalog-v2.md.

================================================================
## 1. PURPOSE / POSTURE
================================================================
TWO COMPLEMENTARY GOALS:
 (1) COACH agents to author better plans via POSITIVE, well-formed guidance — not negative roadblocks. We
     REQUIRE passing a small set of cheap DETERMINISTIC checks, then ADVISE (non-blocking) on where the plan
     falls short. (Research-backed: LLMs can't self-FIND errors but CAN fix them once given the location
     [2311.08516]; rigid semantic gates get churned/reward-hacked [SpecBench 2605.21384]; external well-formed
     feedback beats intrinsic self-critique [2310.01798, 2402.08115, LLM-Modulo 2402.01817].)
 (2) CERTIFY that a review process was followed before implementation. The signed attestation (alongside
     completion-verification + future workflow signatures) is a composable "rigorous agentic development vs
     vibe-coding" signal a CI process can check on a PR.
The review is a PROCESS, not a dialog: it returns a verdict + findings; the CLIENT revises and re-runs to earn a
fresh signature (mirrors completion-verifier). rebar owns review/verdict/findings/signature/invalidation; the
client owns the revise/retry loop.

================================================================
## 2. ARCHITECTURE (two layers)
================================================================
TRIGGER: fires at open->in_progress. `claim` = FAST check for a valid signed attestation; the heavy review runs
OUT-OF-BAND. Routing pivots on HAS_CHILDREN + COMPLEXITY (NOT ticket type — rebar reviews tickets from many
creation styles that are inconsistently typed; DSO's strict type discipline is one style, not the only one).

LAYER 1 — deterministic + structural floor (the ONLY thing that BLOCKS; convergent by construction). Frozen,
unambiguous, cheap, polyglot-where-tooling-exists, fail-open. All-pass is a guaranteed fixed point. Keep it
MINIMAL so there is nothing to game.

LAYER 2 — advisory coaching review (NEVER blocks by default; SURFACES findings authored for remediation). The
judgment criteria + ONE bounded broad open-ended pass for unknown-unknowns. Per-criterion verdicts.

REVIEWER != the model/instance that authored the plan (self-preference). Per-criterion execution is grouped into
chunks per project config (see #6), not one-agent-per-criterion and not a single monolithic pass.

================================================================
## 3. VERDICT MODEL + BLOCKING
================================================================
Per-criterion: PASS / AMBIGUOUS / FAIL. Plus a review-level INDETERMINATE.
- Layer-1 (sound) FAIL -> BLOCKS (facts; near-zero FP; no override needed, though a project may allow one).
- Layer-2 (judgment) FINDINGS -> ADVISORY by default (surface, don't block). AMBIGUOUS = non-blocking ->
  escalate to a higher-tier adjudicator. Reflect "coach, don't penalize."
- PER-CRITERION BLOCKING KNOB (project config): a project may set a Layer-2 criterion to BLOCKING. Blocking
  means BLOCK-WITH-`--force`-OVERRIDE (recorded justification) — friction without deadlock. We do NOT build a
  true no-override hard gate; a project that insists composes one in CI by checking for the signed result (we
  expose the data; not recommended, and the reasons are documented). Sound criteria block without needing an
  override.
- INCOMPLETE (budget cap hit, or a sound tool unavailable): outcome = INDETERMINATE. NOT failed (the cap is
  external to the ticket), NOT certified (the process didn't finish), no signature minted. Whether INDETERMINATE
  BLOCKS `claim` is a PROJECT CONFIG KNOB; DEFAULT = non-blocking (the attestation records "review incomplete";
  `claim` proceeds with that on record). (Tri-state INDETERMINATE / gate-unavailable doctrine: couldn't-decide
  is its own state, never silently a pass.)
NOTE (deferred, follow-ons #1-4): measured-FP surfacing, auto-demote on FP ceiling, confidence/severity floors —
all need measurement tooling + usage data we do not build this epic. v1 ships sensible defaults + the knob; the
instrumentation in #11 is what makes those refinements possible later.

================================================================
## 4. COACHING / FEEDBACK-AUTHORING SPEC (every advisory finding)
================================================================
The reviewer SUPPLIES the finding + location the agent cannot self-detect. Author each finding to this shape
(evidence-backed):
 - GROUND it in a specific criterion/file/section (highest-value lever; 2311.08516, 2510.00777).
 - SPECIFIC + ACTIONABLE, not generic (2303.17651).
 - A concrete SUGGESTED edit ONLY when confident, marked as a suggestion (a wrong fix regresses below none; 2504.06939).
 - SEVERITY-RANK + CAP: a few prioritized findings, critical first; not a firehose (position bias).
 - AFFIRM what already passes (behavior-specific, not bare praise) to suppress over-correction/churn (FlipFlop 2311.08596).
 - Express as a CHECKLIST and require the agent to address each item (TICK/STICK 2410.03608; Rubrics-as-Rewards 2507.17746).
PROMPT TO IMPROVE, NOT PENALIZE: where a signal is missing/weak (e.g. file_impact empty, AC absent), COACH the
agent to add/repair it — no single stored signal is trusted as ground truth; the review validates & improves them.

================================================================
## 5. CRITERIA REGISTRY (extensible; frozen-within-a-loop, grown-between-versions)
================================================================
A criterion = a declarative DESCRIPTOR; the orchestrator is generic. Reuse rebar.llm's catalog.json + contracts
+ prompt + runner. Descriptor: {id, name, scenario, kind(sound|judgment), exec(DET|1-TURN|2-STEP|AGENT),
routing(base|container|leaf|overlay), trigger, tools, checklist(binary where it decomposes cleanly; graded/
narrative where genuinely qualitative), block_policy(advisory[default] | blocking-with-override), config}.
Implement the full criteria set in ONE WAVE, decomposed into appropriately-sized tasks with dependencies.

LAYER 1 (DET, blocks, fail-open): P1 readiness shape (standardized `## Acceptance Criteria` ALL types) · P2
file/symbol/import resolution (LSP/ast-grep) · P3 package existence (syft+registry+LLM-fallback) · P4 oversize
signals · P5 task-DAG validity + cross-task interference · P6 AC/DD quality (compound-AND, vague-lexicon,
spec-fidelity; verify-command robustness — POSIX-shell sub-checks fail-open/advisory off-shell) · P7
destructive/irreversible sniff (escalation signal only).
LAYER 2 (judgment, advisory): F1 measurability & agent-in-session-completability · F4 user/problem present ·
E1 criteria<->description coherence + terminology-consistency + duplicate-requirement detection · E2 ambiguity
+ undercooked-plan (spike/research/TBD) + explicit-decision-deferral · E3 intent fidelity (2-step blind-restate
-> compare) · E5 testing-plan completeness · E6 verification/termination + end-state-reachability · G1+G2
edit-set/scope accuracy (tool-primed; high-blast != fail) · G3 child coverage (4-bucket + 3-condition) [container]
· G4 child consistency (7 categories) [container] · E4 assumption/premise verification (forced codebase probe)
· A1 anti-slop/NIH/config-proliferation/Rule-of-Three.
TRIGGERED OVERLAYS (router-LLM selects relevance; overlay judges adequacy): T1 prior-art/novel-arch · T2
empirical probe · T3 integration feasibility · T4 compat/destructiveness-as-explicit-choice · T5a-e perf/
reliability/security/a11y/maintainability · T6 UX · T7 documentation (+invalidated +hot-path bloat) · T8
LLM/prompt antipatterns · T9 shared-state lifecycle.
CROSS-CUTTING: ONE coherence pass (cross-section contradictions; single structured check, not debate); ONE
bounded broad open-ended pass (unknown-unknowns; advisory; reviewed once per invocation - no internal loop);
single benign-reading FP filter; anti-fabrication instruction ("empty category -> nothing found").
(All borrowed-from-DSO criteria re-implemented in Python, polyglot, as general principles — DSO over-tuned to
its own project; we keep the principle, drop the DSO-specific tuning.)

================================================================
## 6. EXECUTION: CHUNKING, TOOLING, PROPORTIONATE SCRUTINY
================================================================
CHUNKING (project knob): how many criteria batch per LLM call — local/small-model clients chunk finer (reliable,
context-safe), frontier clients chunk coarser (cheaper, comparable quality). DEFAULT determined EMPIRICALLY (NOT
from DSO's rubric): run chunk-size experiments, N repeats each, find the size where result quality degrades;
prime the experiments with DSO tickets as realistic inputs.
TOOLING (fail-open is an IRONCLAD invariant): solidlsp (Serena, already in repo) + ast-grep (P2/G1/G2); syft +
per-ecosystem registry + LLM-fallback (P3); scc + lizard (P4). Unsupported lang / no server / crash / timeout /
no shim -> skipped = PASS, NEVER FAIL; hard "missing" only from top-tier servers. (This is the only reason the
DET tier generalizes across the diverse client base.)
PROPORTIONATE SCRUTINY: review depth scales with COMPLEXITY + has_children (small task = DET + a light pass;
epic/has_children = full depth). ESCALATION to a stronger model when tier=deep AND (blast-radius high OR
centrality fan-in high OR diff large). COMPUTE + persist centrality + blast-radius at plan time (cheap;
feeds escalation now and seeds the tuning corpus later). RISK POSTURE is project-configurable with a sensible
default; the project owns the time/cost/risk tradeoff. BUDGET CAP per plan (wall-clock + cost); spend at chunk
granularity, checkpoint between chunks (no half-criterion waste), order chunks by priority so a cap sheds the
least-valuable criteria.

================================================================
## 7. CONVERGENCE
================================================================
The only loop is the CLIENT re-invoking after revising. Convergence is FREE because Layer 1 (deterministic,
frozen) is the sole blocker -> all-pass is a guaranteed fixed point regardless of advisory findings. On a re-run
with a changed plan, review the current plan FRESH (do not limit to prior findings). diff-only re-review is an
OPTIONAL cost-saver, not a convergence mechanism. No internal refine loop; no second broad pass within a review.

================================================================
## 8. ATTESTATION / CERTIFICATION
================================================================
The signed attestation = "we ran our process to the best of our ability given this codebase, and no red flags
were raised" — NOT "this plan is perfect." Records COVERAGE (which sound checks ran vs skipped-unsupported) for
transparency. Bound to the latest MATERIAL event commit on the tickets branch (scope (i)): edits to description /
AC / file_impact / decomposition INVALIDATE (re-certify); tags/comments/links/assignee do NOT (no re-review
treadmill). JUSTIFICATION: the cert asserts process-followed-against-THIS-plan; honoring it after a material edit
would falsely certify content never reviewed. The cert is the composable rigor signal for CI (review +
completion-verification + future workflows).

================================================================
## 9. OBSERVABILITY / FP-ANALYSIS SUBSTRATE (cheap, in-scope; the enabler for follow-ons #1-4)
================================================================
Decouple cheap CAPTURE (now) from FP ANALYSIS (offline, later). Leverage event-sourcing.
STORAGE MODEL (decided): TWO artifacts with opposite needs.
 (a) ATTESTATION/SIGNATURE = a NORMAL reduced event ON the ticket (head state; what `claim` checks; reuses the
     close-gate SIGNATURE machinery / signing.py).
 (b) REVIEW_RESULT (bulky observability) = a reducer-IGNORED SIDECAR EVENT on the ticket — stored in the ticket's
     own event stream (free auto-commit/push + conflict-free union-merge + co-located with the events the FP
     analysis must correlate against), but NOT folded into head/.cache and NOT seen by deps/validate/health
     (the session_log out-of-hot-paths treatment applied at the EVENT-TYPE level; the one new bit of machinery =
     the reducer intentionally SKIPS this type, making the existing preserved-and-ignored behavior deliberate).
     REJECTED: a single shared ledger file (line-level merge conflicts in a multi-clone auto-pushed store -
     breaks rebar's per-event-file union-merge convergence). ALTERNATIVE/fallback: a separate per-review-FILE
     store / `reviews` branch (conflict-safe, keeps work-ticket dirs pristine) - but it is a new parallel store
     AND forces a cross-store join; only switch if ticket-dir growth becomes a real problem. NOT one-ticket-per-
     review (heavy ticket-space pollution + relates_to-join instead of co-location).
     Keep the body COMPACT (fingerprints + per-criterion verdicts + short finding text + citation; full plan
     text recoverable from the bound material commit, never duplicated); old REVIEW_RESULT events are
     compactable/archivable after offline extraction (observability, not state).
The REVIEW_RESULT record carries: {review_id, ticket_id, ts, bound material-commit SHA, model+version, depth tier, chunking
config + criterion->chunk map, budget cap/spent, budget-hit; per-criterion: criterion_id, verdict, kind, exec,
blocking-configured, finding text+citation, FINDING FINGERPRINT = hash(criterion + normalized location +
normalized text), model self-reported confidence [captured-but-untrusted, for later study]; overall outcome,
signature-minted; prior review_id if a re-review}. Log `--force` overrides structured (which criteria, reason).
LEAN ON EXISTING EVENTS for the rest (no new tracking): EDIT/material commits, STATUS/reopen/supersede,
completion-verification result. OFFLINE a replay+join (on ticket_id + fingerprints) computes per criterion:
fire rate, REMEDIATION RATE (fingerprint resolved after a revision = the real "is the advice useful" metric),
FP proxy (flagged -> overridden/ignored -> ticket closes clean/CV-PASS/no rework), FN proxy (silent ->
later reworked), chunk-size quality experiments, confidence-vs-correctness, depth-tier distribution. CAPTURE
ONLY — never aggregate/decide in-system; no human-feedback requirement (optional later).

================================================================
## 10. TUNING METHODOLOGY (DSO corpus = simulation/priming fixture, NOT a threshold source)
================================================================
rebar != DSO; DSO bands won't transfer. Use the DSO corpus to: (a) ground "how do we review THIS kind of ticket"
on representative real examples; (b) SIMULATE candidate rules over the corpus to see how they'd redistribute
tickets across review levels (over/under-review check); (c) prime the chunk-size + threshold experiments with
real inputs. The DSO complexity labels are unvalidated evaluator OUTPUT — one input among many, never ground
truth. Set our defaults from experiments + simulation, then re-derive per client from live REVIEW_RESULT data.

================================================================
## 11. FOLLOW-ON REGISTER (tracked SEPARATELY from this epic)
================================================================
F1. FP/dismissal + remediation-rate TRACKING across review cycles (the offline analysis tooling on #9 data).
F2. AUTO-DEMOTE a blocking criterion past an FP ceiling (depends on F1).
F3. CONFIDENCE/SEVERITY floor for blocking findings (fine-tuning on usage data).
F4. PROMOTE more checks to blocking-by-default as low-FP ones are observed (depends on F1).
F5. Project CONFIGURATION TOOL/UX (risk posture, thresholds, chunking, per-criteria blocking).
F6. BUG-ticket review (bugs shipped SKIPPED; dedicated follow-on epic; different shape from planning tickets).
F7. Cross-ticket NO-OVERLAP / DEPENDENCY-AWARE *semantic* detection (deterministic file-impact/link overlap is
    in-scope; semantic backlog-scan duplication -> follow-on).
F8. Project-SUPPLIED review criteria (registry future seam) + PROJECT-INVARIANT compliance (house rules).
F9. CODE-DRIFT invalidation (re-review when referenced code changes underneath; scope (i) now, drift later).
F10. CALIBRATED ABSTENTION + a labeled GOLD set (if/when attainable).
F11. RIGOR-SIGNATURE CHAIN / CI integration beyond exposing the signed result (helpers, conventions, more
     workflow signatures).
F12. SEVERITY-MINOR-FLOOR + NOT-FLAG tuning (per-criterion "what's minor"; needs usage data).
(In-scope-but-improves-later: centrality + blast-radius computed at plan time for escalation now; persisted as a
labeled tuning corpus = the follow-on benefit.)

================================================================
## 12. OPEN / PENDING APPROVAL
================================================================
- Proportionate-depth thresholds + escalation weights (default to be set from experiment/simulation; Joe approves posture).
- Budget posture (per-plan cap; INDETERMINATE-blocks-claim default = non-blocking; Joe approves).
- Confirm bug-skip + follow-on epic (F6).
- NEXT after sign-off: decompose this design into the rebar implementation epic (one wave, tasks with deps),
  tracked in rebar; create the follow-on register items (F1-F12) as separate linked tickets (discovered_from).
